fields.description:
## Why
A significant epic failure showed leaf plans DRIFT as leaf tickets are claimed and edited: by epic close, the leaf ACs described a very different plan than what the epic said would be delivered, and nothing caught the divergence until the epic's completion verification. No current criterion checks a leaf plan against its PARENT'S plan — G1G2/E4 ground against the codebase, COH checks internal coherence. IMPORTANT CORRECTION (verified against the code): there is NO existing "ancestor carve-out" that injects a parent's PLAN TEXT — `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` (pass1.py:281) injects only the parent's ticket ID string into context. So injecting the parent's actual plan text is NEW work this ticket must build, not a mechanism to mirror. The review that gates the CLAIM is the right moment to catch drift, ticket by ticket, instead of at epic close.

## What
- Add a new plan-review criterion (working id `G7`, facet parent-containment) to src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json + the registry: `exec: AGENT` (it must read the parent ticket from the store, like the other codebase-grounded criteria), with a rubric prompt at src/rebar/llm/reviewers/ following the existing G-series prompt conventions.
- ROUTING — fires ONLY on leaf tickets: applies_at gates on (ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children). Bugs/session_logs stay exempt as today; parentless tickets and containers never run it.
- SEMANTICS: the leaf's What/Scope/ACs must be a SUBSET of the parent's declared scope — a finding fires when the leaf (a) delivers something the parent's plan does not contain or implies is out of scope, (b) contradicts a parent AC/success criterion, or (c) redefines a deliverable the parent specifies differently. Consistent narrowing (a leaf doing PART of the parent) is NOT a finding.
- CONFLICT RULE (the operator's directive, encoded in the rubric AND the Pass-4 coaching move): the PARENT WINS — it holds the full delivery context. Coaching directs the agent to realign the leaf to the parent; if the agent believes the parent is genuinely wrong, the productive move is to update the PARENT first (which stales the parent's own attestation and forces its re-review) and only then re-review the leaf — never to silently diverge the leaf.
- CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism — named edit targets): extend the plan-review context so the criterion receives the parent's title + description (and the grandparent's, one level up, if present), BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps). Concretely: add a `parent_plan` field (parent + grandparent title/description, bounded) to `det_floor.PlanContext` (det_floor.py:95); populate it in `orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached` (orchestrator.py:163) by reading the parent chain from the store; and surface it into the G7 criterion's context via `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` (pass1.py:281) alongside the parent-ID string it already emits. This is additive — existing criteria that don't consume `parent_plan` are unaffected.
- Pass-2/3 stay untouched: findings flow through the standard verify -> decide pipeline; severity maps onto the existing plan axes (divergent_implementation is the natural home — a leaf diverging from its parent is exactly a divergent-implementation signal).
- POSTURE: ship `default_posture: advisory` at 0.95 per the ADR-0036 expand-contract house pattern, BUT record fire-rate + adjudication from day one. IN-SESSION deliverable (the promotion-decision PATH, not the decision itself): document, as a comment on this ticket at merge time, (a) the exact query to compute G7's fire-rate + validity from the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars, (b) the promotion trigger — blocking at the plan-review 0.6–0.7 band once the observation window shows an acceptable false-fire rate — and (c) a pointer to where the actual promotion decision will be recorded. The actual promotion decision depends on post-deployment field data and is explicitly DEFERRED (out of this ticket's close path; it belongs with the epic's deferred measurement follow-up 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d).
- NOTE (explicit non-goal here, candidate follow-up): a material PARENT edit does not currently stale the CHILDREN'S plan-review attestations, so drift introduced by editing the epic after leaves are reviewed is only caught on the leaf's next re-review. If the observation window shows parent-edit drift, file a follow-up for parent-edit-propagating staleness in attest.compute_validity.

## Scope
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json, src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py (register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM/AGENT_TIER/CODEBASE_GROUNDED; applies_at leaf gate), a new reviewers/plan_review_G7.md rubric prompt, the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py), the context-assembly path for the NEW parent-plan injection — det_floor.py `PlanContext` (add `parent_plan` field), orchestrator.py `_assemble_context_uncached` (populate it from the parent chain), pass1.py `_ticket_graph_blob` (surface it) — the regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md, and tests (routing: fires on leaf-with-parent only, skipped for parentless/container/bug/session_log; semantics: fixture parent/leaf pairs — contained/exceeding/contradicting; a test asserting parent_plan context assembly; posture pin; validate_packaged_routing()==[]). No kernel decide.py/verify.py math changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The criterion runs ONLY on tickets that have a parent_id and no children; a routing test proves it is skipped for parentless tickets, containers, bugs, and session_logs.
- [ ] Fixture-driven tests: a leaf narrowing its parent PASSES; a leaf adding out-of-parent-scope deliverables fires; a leaf contradicting a parent AC fires.
- [ ] The rubric and the Pass-4 coaching move state the parent-wins rule and direct remediation toward realigning the leaf or updating the parent FIRST — never silently diverging the leaf.
- [ ] The criterion ships advisory (0.95) with fire-rate observable in REVIEW_RESULT sidecars. In-session proof: a comment on this ticket documents the promotion-decision PATH — the fire-rate/validity query, the 0.6–0.7 promotion band + trigger, and where the decision is recorded. The actual promotion decision is explicitly deferred (see 72b6), not required to close this ticket.
- [ ] The parent's plan text is injected into the criterion context (bounded to parent + grandparent), with a test asserting the assembly.
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.
